DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF EMILY DAVIDSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE SUDDEN MEDICAL EMERGENCY DEFENSE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO RESPOND TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS 5, 6, 7, AND 8 FOR THE PAST 5 YEARS OF MEDICAL RECORDS AND ALL MEDICAL RECORDS EVIDENCING ANY NEUROLOGICAL SYMPTOMS OR DISORDERS. DEFENDANT MUST ALSO PROVIDE PSYCHOLOGICAL OR PSYCHIATRIC RECORDS FOR 5 YEARS PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT. THE COURT SPECIFIED, HOWEVER, THIS ORDER IS FOR RECORDS AS THEY RELATE TO ISSUES WITH FATHER AND CANCER. RECORDS ARE TO BE SEPARATED, SEALED AND TURNED OVER TO COUNSEL TO PROVIDE TO THE DOCTOR. IF THE DOCTOR DEEMS THE RECORDS RELEVANT, THE RECORDS WILL BE SUBJECT TO A PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR ATTORNEY EYES ONLY. INTERROGATORY RESPONSES ARE DUE BY SEPTEMBER 1, 2011. THE COURT TOOK PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ANOTHER DEPOSITION UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO REOPEN DEPOSITION OF DEFENDANT IS DENIED. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

DEFENDANT LINDA GORDAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 (302)

Notice Of Motion For Neurological Examination
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF EMILY DAVIDSON'S MOTION FOR NEUROLOGICAL EXAMINATION.
Appearances: Jeremy M. Jessup of the Law Offices of Cholakian & Associates (650.871.9544) for defendant and responding party; Spencer J. Pahlke of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (981.7210) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for a neurological examination is granted. The plaintiff's expert is authorized to delve into areas of inquiry outlined in the court's ruling on the motion to compel. The court granted defendant's request as to asking questions about the accident. The request to limit the doctor's exam is denied. Plaintiff to submit a proposed of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Discovery Related To The Sudden Medical Emergency Defense
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF EMILY DAVIDSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RELATED TO THE SUDDEN MEDICAL EMERGENCY DEFENSE.
Appearances: Jeremy M. Jessup of the Law Offices of Cholakian & Associates (650.871.9544) for defendant and responding party; Spencer J. Pahlke of Walkup Melodia Kelly & Schoenberger (981.7210) for plaintiff and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted in part. Defendant is required to respond to request for production of documents 5, 6, 7, and 8 for the past 5 years of medical records and all medical records evidencing any neurological symptoms or disorders. Defendant must also provide psychological or psychiatric records for 5 years prior to the accident. The court specified, however, this order is for records as they relate to issues with father and cancer. Records are to be separated, sealed and turned over to counsel to provide to the doctor. If the doctor deems the records relevant, the records will be subject to a protective order for attorney eyes only. Interrogatory responses are due by September 1, 2011. The court took plaintiff's request for another deposition under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: plaintiff's request to reopen deposition of defendant is denied. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order.
